item.title: Peace talks present big test on global stage for JD Vance
item.description: Tehran and Washington have never held direct bilateral negotiations

item.title: The damage wrought on the Middle East’s oil and gas supplies
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Qatar have both suffered significant damage to production capacity during the US-Israeli war against Iran

item.title: Only two events have mattered in my 30 years of investing
item.description: Markets have shrugged off everything from geopolitics to financial crises

item.title: Isa season flops as UK retail investors shun falling stock market
item.description: Buyers stayed away during the crucial annual buying period amid pessimism over geopolitical tensions and the global economy

item.title: Inheritance tax weighs on business owners: ‘Will it all be taken away in the end?’
item.description: From this week, many businesses can no longer be passed to the next generation free of tax Tax

item.title: BP’s proxy fight exposes bugs in shareholder voting machine
item.description: On contentious matters, investors are less likely to follow proxies

item.title: Jacob’s Creek should come in smaller bottles
item.description: Relaunch of popular 1990s tipple might need more than nostalgia to stand out from today’s crowd

item.title: Mortgage borrowers seek shorter-term deals as market volatility saps confidence
item.description: Flexibility is the watchword among UK borrowers amid signs of slowing activity in the housing market

item.title: UK households turn to solar panels as energy bills bite
item.description: Fallout from Middle East crisis prompts more people to explore the alternative source of power

item.title: Separating the signal from the noise in private credit
item.description: What matters most is how capital was deployed and underwritten

item.title: Tofoo co-founder: ‘I’d lost £250,000 in investments before I started the company’
item.description: It was fourth time lucky for entrepreneur David Knibbs

item.title: Anthropic closes in on OpenAI as US business use surges
item.description: Divergence reflects company’s recent rapid growth owing to strong interest in its Claude Code products

item.title: The next matcha: coffee chains bet on ube’s viral appeal
item.description: Costa and Starbucks launch purple drinks in effort to recreate viral success of matcha among young consumers

item.title: ‘Fortress China’ shows cracks as Iran war strains supply chains
item.description: Energy, chemicals and helium supply under pressure despite Beijing’s push to build up economic security

item.title: Clients’ barrage of AI-generated queries risks pushing up lawyers’ fees
item.description: Firms may raise prices for fixed-fee contracts if clients keep sending flurries of emails and letters

item.title: Does the war in Iran signal a structural shift for energy markets?
item.description: Countries that rely on Middle Eastern crude – particularly those in Asia – are grappling with increasing paucity of oil and gas

item.title: Lebanon and Israel agree to talks in Washington next week
item.description: The talks would be the first direct negotiations between the two countries in more than four decades

item.title: Italy’s Eni takes stake in Canada’s Nouveau Monde Graphite
item.description: Energy giant will take board seat as critical mineral producer moves closer to construction of Quebec graphite mine

item.title: Molotov cocktail thrown at home of OpenAI chief Sam Altman
item.description: Suspect arrested outside AI start-up’s San Francisco headquarters after threatening to burn it down

item.title: Trump touts Palantir’s ‘war’ role after short seller attack
item.description: US president’s endorsement follows criticism from famed investor Michael Burry

item.title: Cyber security stocks fall on worries over Anthropic’s advanced AI tool
item.description: Mythos model was able to detect critical software vulnerabilities that were missed by legacy systems

item.title: Canadian bank execs, regulators meet to discuss risks raised by Anthropic’s new AI model
item.description: Anthropic has said its Mythos model has already found thousands of cybersecurity vulnerabilities, raising concerns about its use by threat actors

item.title: Lloyds will not take legal action against UK’s £9bn car finance redress scheme
item.description: Britain’s largest provider of vehicle loans wants to draw a line under scandal that rocked the sector

item.title: Trump has broken something, and the prewar economy may never return
item.description: Regardless of whether the ceasefire with Iran holds, the risks of a catastrophe remain

item.title: Pitching in: Offering housing and schooling for at-risk boys in Kenya
item.description: When Fred Hacker took over Life4Kids Canada a decade ago, the non-profit was on the verge of closing down

item.title: AI firm Cohere in merger talks with Germany’s Aleph Alpha, sources say
item.description: Merger would be politically sensitive as Cohere is a crucially important company for Canada’s AI ambitions

item.title: Iran-linked ships dominate Hormuz traffic after ceasefire
item.description: At least nine of the 14 or so vessels to have transited chokepoint since the pause in fighting have ties to Tehran

item.title: Why has the UK economy been so badly hit by the Iran war?
item.description: Over-reliance on imported gas and worries over Starmer’s future likely to stall pick-up in momentum, analysts say

item.title: Crispin Odey abandons £79mn libel case against the FT over sexual misconduct claims
item.description: The FT had said 15 women were willing to testify in court against former hedge fund boss

item.title: Partners Group backs gating as private credit redemptions test liquidity
item.description: Swiss-listed private capital group seeks to ‘offer transparency’ with update against ‘complex’ market backdrop

item.title: U.S.-Canada lumber dispute is a ‘broken process,’ B.C. wood manufacturers say
item.description: U.S. plans to reduce duty rates on Canadian softwood but association warns of uncertainty on final rate

item.title: Ireland risks cutting off oil supply amid fuel protests, premier warns
item.description: Micheál Martin’s comments come as demonstrations threaten to spill over into Northern Ireland

item.title: Egypt is the bellwether for a global debt crisis
item.description: The country, highly indebted and suffering from double-digit inflation, is fully exposed to price shockwaves unleashed by the Iran war

item.title: US inflation jumps to two-year high as Iran war ripples across economy
item.description: Consumer sentiment sinks to record low as petrol prices post record monthly surge in March

item.title: European stocks are the losers in the Iran war fallout
item.description: The US market is more immune from shocks given still healthy American economic conditions and earnings

item.title: Directors’ Deals: 3i boss backs US ambitions
item.description: Private equity group’s share price down by 40 per cent in the past six months

item.title: Middle East conflict drains Singapore’s marine fuel supplies
item.description: Growing shipping congestion around the world’s biggest refuelling port shrinks reserves

item.title: U.S. consumer sentiment dives to a record low in April amid Iran war
item.description: Survey notes almost all the responses came before a ceasefire agreement earlier this week

item.title: Rare US diesel armada heads to Australia in bid to ease fuel crunch
item.description: Armada of tankers sailing from as far away as the US and UK as reliance on imports from Asia in focus during supply crunch

item.title: The biggest IPO in history has a hole in its safety net
item.description: RIP Section 11?

item.title: U.S. inflation surges in March on soaring gas prices due to war in Iran
item.description: Consumer prices rose 3.3% from a year earlier; on a monthly basis, prices rose 0.9%, the largest such increase in nearly four years

item.title: Vacancy tax changes, break-ins rock Toronto’s Rosedale, the Home of the Week and more top real estate stories
item.description: Plus, this week’s lowest fixed and variable mortgage rates in Canada

item.title: Corus Entertainment awaits approval of recapitalization plan, quarterly revenue down 15%
item.description: Chief executive John Gossling said the timing for those steps to be completed remains unclear, as it depends largely on the regulatory process

item.title: Data Dive with Nik Nanos: Canada must embrace a culture of ambition and risk-taking
item.description: The country’s ability to remake and renew the economy hinges on whether entrepreneurs act, not on rhetoric

item.title: Annuities look more attractive in wake of inflation fears, say advisers
item.description: Benefits of a fixed and stable income set to play more prominent role in retirement planning

item.title: U.S. trade court weighs legality of Trump’s 10% global tariff
item.description: Several states and small businesses say it sidesteps a Supreme Court ruling that invalidated most of U.S. President’s previous tariffs

item.title: Home swapping can make travel more affordable and easier for families
item.description: As the rising cost of fuel makes flying pricier, home swapping is a good way to pay less for accommodations

item.title: The untold danger of unchecked capitalism
item.description: Economic policy often assumes that individuals are rational, emotionless actors - but people don’t always behave the way markets expect them to

item.title: AI has become my daughter’s third parent
item.description: The post AI has become my daughter’s third parent appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Why did BlackBerry surprise investors this week? Take our business and investing news quiz
item.description: Test your recollection of the top stories of the week ending April 10

item.title: Canada sees modest employment growth in March, jobless rate unchanged at 6.7%
item.description: The economy added 14,100 jobs, compared with an estimated of 15,000

item.title: Six lessons for investors on pricing disaster
item.description: Buy The Dip mantras ignore the fact that not all US market vulnerabilities have been accounted for Gillian Tett

item.title: More than half of the US in drought after near-record March temperatures
item.description: Global average monthly rise sustained at 1.48C since pre-industrial times

item.title: Iran won. Almost everyone else lost
item.description: The U.S., the Gulf states and Israel will pay for the conflict and its outcome

item.title: The American exceptionalism trade is under pressure as valuation premium for U.S. stocks shrinks
item.description: The significant gap between price-to-earnings ratios over past 10 years has been narrowing since October

item.title: The green, the cheese, the money behind the moon: A new space economy dawns
item.description: The space race is no longer about politics or bragging rights

item.title: The mortgage renewal wave didn’t destroy finances. But how badly are they hurting?
item.description: We want to hear from readers on how higher interest rates are affecting them

item.title: Canada, U.S., U.K. seize more than $12-million in crypto scam proceeds in Operation Atlantic
item.description: Law-enforcement agencies plan to probe additional US$33-million potentially linked to fraud schemes

item.title: U.S. software stocks fall as Anthropic’s new AI model revives disruption fears
item.description: Company restricted access to ‘Claude Mythos’ AI model to about 40 companies over cybersecurity concerns

item.title: European insurance regulators push for €10bn-plus disaster fund
item.description: Insurance gap grows as only ‘a fraction’ of losses are covered

item.title: The pros and cons of stretch goals
item.description: Setting really tough targets encourages risk-taking, for good and bad

item.title: Japan’s mighty carmakers are in serious trouble
item.description: They will need bold thinking to survive

item.title: A giant succession wave is coming for family businesses
item.description: Many will not survive

item.title: U.S. economy grew a sluggish 0.5% annually in fourth quarter
item.description: U.S. GDP decelerated after posting impressive growth of 4.4% in the previous quarter

item.title: U.S. PCE inflation picks up in February, seen rising further in March
item.description: Index rose 0.4 per cent, on par with expectations, and 2.8 per cent over 12 months

item.title: EU to relax methane rules to secure energy supplies
item.description: Proposed changes offer ‘flexibilities’ to strict requirements due to be imposed on fossil fuel importers

item.title: The climate showdown between Big Oil and US states
item.description: Vermont and New York’s ‘climate superfund’ laws face legal challenges and a pending Supreme Court case

item.title: Milei win as Argentina reviews glacier law to boost mining
item.description: Legal battle looms as Congress backs overhaul of environmental protection rule

item.title: One neat trick to end extreme poverty
item.description: An age-old problem might have a surprisingly simple solution

item.title: Can the secondary market allay private-credit fears?
item.description: In the short run, a bit. In the long run, a lot more

item.description: A a coup, a conspiracy and even a (possibly unrelated) corpse

item.title: South Korea’s AI industrial policy meets the energy shock
item.description: The collision will not be pretty

item.title: The U.S. isn’t happy about Canada’s quest for digital sovereignty
item.description: Countries are converging on laws that grant them greater control over where their data lives and who has access

item.title: Iran still controls the Strait of Hormuz, and nothing will ever be the same again
item.description: The war has changed the complexion of the global energy equation, with Iran emerging as the new pivot in the emerging new order

item.description: Residual risks and ruined infrastructure will keep prices high

item.title: In today’s savings and GIC accounts, locking your money away for longer is not much better
item.description: Despite slightly higher rates, five-year terms offer little advantage over short-term savings

item.title: Federal Reserve minutes show growing openness to rate hikes at March meeting
item.description: A developing global oil shock was in its third week when the Fed met on March 17-18
